summary: Run a narrow weak-pi pulsed ODMR refresh on image231924_c01 after the terminal Ramsey scout showed a dominant 1.59 MHz component.
scientific_rationale: The terminal Ramsey scout has a real oscillatory signal but the dominant 1.593 MHz FFT bin can be either lower-13C-sideband-like structure or residual microwave-center detuning within the earlier weak-pi +/-1 MHz uncertainty. A bounded narrow weak-pi pODMR around the previous center is the safest next calibration before a stronger T2star/13C claim or Ramsey repeat.
comparison_targets: previous weak-pi pODMR center 3.8758666667 GHz +/- ~1 MHz
Ramsey expected features: det 2.000 MHz and estimated 13C sidebands 1.616/2.384 MHz
Doherty et al. Physics Reports 528, 1-45 (2013), DOI 10.1016/j.physrep.2013.02.001, local library/Crossref metadata as general NV reference
expected_evidence: terminal narrow weak-pi pODMR bridge result with savedexperiment path
raw/readout-aware line-shape review with refined center or explicit no-refinement status
decision whether the next Ramsey should use corrected mw_freq or a det-shift diagnostic
success_criteria: bridge queue idle before submission and pre-enqueue advisory has no hard blockers
acquisition completes a 29-point, 7 MHz weak-pi window centered on 3.8758666667 GHz
raw/readout-aware review reduces center/detuning ambiguity enough to plan the next Ramsey follow-up
stop_conditions: bridge queue is not idle immediately before submission
project lifecycle is canceled or do_not_submit_or_mutate_bridge_queue is true
verifier or pre-enqueue advisory reports hard blockers
auto-align counts collapse or bridge execution fails before acquiring data
bridge_action: execute pulsed_odmr_rabimodulated_v1 weak-pi pODMR via managed batch path

target.seed_source: latest terminal Ramsey auto-align tracked position in result.json

planned_measurement.analysis_plan: raw export and readout-aware center review first; no automatic bridge fit
